data.title: Inventor from Scottsdale, Ariz., creates RE - BAR attachment for strength training machines.
data.description: In Scottsdale, Arizona, on July 25, 2025, an inventor shared their inspiration behind creating a new accessory for strength-training equipment. The inventor introduced the revolutionary RE - BAR, aimed at facilitating a range of exercises efficiently. This innovative design caters to diverse workout needs.

data.title: Dragonfly defends DOJ scrutiny over Tornado Cash investment, ready to fight back against potential legal action.
data.description: A venture capital firm known as Dragonfly Capital, by making an early investment in Tornado Cash, may face legal consequences from the Department of Justice. This decision has put the firm at risk of potential legal action. The specifics of the investment and potential repercussions have garnered attention, with the involvement of the DOJ adding a significant layer of complexity to the situation. This highlights the potential ramifications of investing in such projects, with Dragonfly Capital now in a precarious position due to their investment choice.

data.title: Spear AI receives seed funding from Scare the Bear Capital & Cortical Ventures to boost maritime AI solutions.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, in Washington, Spear AI, a prominent creator of maritime artificial intelligence technologies, revealed the successful finalization of its seed funding round. This round gathered investments from Scare the Bear Capital and Cortical Ventures. The funding secured will assist the company in further developing its innovative solutions and expanding its reach in the maritime sector.

data.title: Former BlackRock exec, key in launching Bitcoin, Ethereum ETFs, joins SharpLink Gaming as co-CEO. Exciting news for both companies.
data.description: The head of cryptocurrency at BlackRock, a prominent financial company, is departing to assume the role of co-CEO at SharpLink Gaming, which manages the treasury for Ethereum. This significant move reflects the growing influence of cryptocurrency in the financial industry.

data.title: Indiana State University partners with Thompson Thrift, renaming construction management department in a historic collaboration.
data.description: In a significant development on July 25, 2025, in Terre Haute, Indiana, Thompson Thrift, a prominent name in the real estate sector, made a historic donation to Indiana State University. This generous gift aims to foster innovation, enhance educational opportunities, and promote collaboration within the construction management field. The partnership between the university and Thompson Thrift is set to revolutionize the industry, with a focus on advancing key areas such as research, training, and practical application. This initiative signifies a new chapter in construction management, with the potential to impact not only academia but also the wider business landscape. Through this collaboration, both entities aim to drive growth, excellence, and transformation in the construction sector, setting new benchmarks for industry engagement and progress.

data.title: BTC, ETH, XRP, BNB, SOL, DOGE, ADA, HYPE, XLM, SUI at risk as Bitcoin may drop below $115,000, halting the altcoin season.
data.description: The support level for Bitcoin is currently at $115,000, with the possibility of a breakout that could halt the ongoing altcoin season.

data.title: KPN completes €250m share buyback, repurchasing 62,465,117 shares at an average price of €4 per share.
data.description: KPN has recently finalized a share buyback program worth €250 million, which began on February 25, 2025, and concluded on July 25, 2025. During this period, the company successfully repurchased 62,465,117 ordinary shares at an average price of €4.00 per share.

data.title: Inventor from Glendale, Ariz. develops product named THE STYLER to hold/grip small makeup pencils. Published on July 25, 2025.
data.description: On July 25, 2025, in Pittsburgh, an inventor from Glendale, Arizona shared their inspiration behind creating THE STYLER, a new innovation aimed at improving the grip and usability of small makeup pencils. The inventor highlighted the intention to address the common issue of small pencils becoming difficult to use over time. THE STYLER boasts a patent-pending design that eliminates the need to dispose of pencils prematurely due to size constraints, ultimately offering a more sustainable solution for makeup users.

data.title: Congress urges FDA to stop illegal weight-loss drug imports from China, including counterfeit GLP-1 drugs like Wegovy.
data.description: A bipartisan group of over 80 members, led by U.S. Representative Richard Hudson and Herbert Conaway, penned a letter to FDA Commissioner Marty Makary requesting action against counterfeit and copycat versions of GLP-1 drugs. The letter, delivered on Friday, particularly highlighted the influx of fake versions of Novo Nordisk A/S' Wegovy (semaglutide) and Eli Lilly and Co.'s Zepbound entering the market. These illicit products, which also include unauthorized versions of Ozempic and Wegovy's active ingredients, are often smuggled from unregistered foreign sources, mainly China. The group emphasized the urgent need to address this growing threat of counterfeit anti-obesity medications in the U.S. For the complete story, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Dow Jones Futures saw gains for Circle, CoreWeave, Palantir, Roblox, Shopify. CPI inflation report expect Tuesday.
data.description: Stock futures for the Dow Jones showed significant gains on Monday for companies such as Circle, CoreWeave, Palantir, Roblox, and Shopify. These companies emerged as the biggest winners. Investors are now eagerly awaiting the CPI inflation report scheduled to be released on Tuesday. The upcoming release of this important economic data will be closely watched by market participants.

data.title: Federal regulators are proposing to reduce a key capital requirement for big banks after years of planning.
data.description: Federal regulators are considering reducing a significant capital requirement for large banks that has been under review for several years.

data.title: China reduced its U.S. Treasury holdings to $756.3 billion, lowest in 16 years, signaling displeasure amidst trade tensions.
data.description: China has reduced its U.S. Treasury holdings to $756.3 billion, the lowest in over 16 years in May. This is the third consecutive month of reductions, down from over $1.3 trillion held between 2012 and 2016. Because of this reduction, China has slipped to third place among foreign creditors to the U.S., behind Japan and the United Kingdom. This move is likely a geopolitical signal, possibly in response to Trump's aggressive tariff regime, serving as a form of financial retaliation. By lessening their American debt holdings, China is subtly reminding the U.S. that economic pressure can be applied quietly through financial markets. This demonstrates that China has leverage beyond just trade in their relationship with the U.S.
